name: Ketamine
description: Dissociative anesthetic providing analgesia, sedation, and amnesia while preserving airway reflexes and hemodynamic stability. Preferred analgesic in TCCC -- maintains BP unlike opioids. Also used for procedural sedation and RSI.
dose: Analgesia: 0.1-0.3 mg/kg IV/IO (slow push) | 0.5 mg/kg IM. Procedural sedation: 1-2 mg/kg IV | 4-6 mg/kg IM. RSI induction: 1-2 mg/kg IV.
warnings: Emergence reactions (delirium/hallucinations) -- mitigate with midazolam 1-2mg IV if available.
Laryngospasm rare but possible -- keep BVM available.

pediatric.dose: Analgesia: 0.5 mg/kg IV. Procedural sedation: 1-2 mg/kg IV | 4-5 mg/kg IM.
pediatric.note: Well-studied in pediatrics. Preferred for painful procedures.

obstetric.note: Safe for maternal analgesia at low doses. May cause neonatal respiratory depression at high doses if delivered near time of delivery.

source: JTS CPG: Acute Pain Management / TCCC Guidelines
